title: 1x3 Blanket Ladder
summary: A leaning blanket ladder built from seven 1x3 furring strips — two long rails and five rungs, screwed together with no angled cuts or joinery at all.
description:
This is about as beginner-friendly as a ladder gets: two long 1x3 rails and five short rungs, all cut with plain square crosscuts — no angles, no notches, no pocket holes required. Lay the two rails on a flat surface, mark evenly spaced rung positions along both, and screw or nail each rung between them.

**Consistent rung spacing is the only thing that matters here** — measure once, mark both rails together if possible, and double-check spacing before driving any screws, since an uneven ladder is the one thing that would make this simple build look sloppy.

Lean the finished ladder against a wall at a comfortable angle and drape blankets or towels over the rungs. No wall anchoring is required since the ladder simply leans, but keep the base far enough out that it doesn't slip.

materials.name: Self-tapping star bit screws, 1-1/2", or brad nails, 1-1/4", and glue
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Mark the rung positions
steps.body: Lay the two rails flat and mark evenly spaced rung positions on both together.

steps.title: Attach the rungs
steps.body: Screw or nail each rung between the two rails at the marked positions.
materials: Self-tapping star bit screws, 1-1/2", or brad nails, 1-1/4", and glue

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: 1x3 blanket ladder leaning against a wall
